title: Day 09 Quiz — Debouncing, Custom Hooks, and Search
description: Verify debouncing, timers, generic API types, custom hooks, and controlled search patterns.

questions.prompt: Debouncing triggers an action on every keystroke immediately.
questions.explanation: Debouncing waits until input stops changing for a specified delay before firing the action.

questions.prompt: Why extract useDebounce instead of inline setTimeout in the component?

options.text: React requires all timers in custom hooks
Reusable logic, testable in isolation, cleaner components
setTimeout does not work inside components
Custom hooks are faster than inline code

questions.explanation: Custom hooks extract reusable stateful logic — the same reason you use useState instead of manual DOM updates.

options.text: A React component that renders pagination
A generic API wrapper where T is the item type in results
A TypeScript enum for page numbers
A Redux slice for paginated data

questions.explanation: Generics let one interface describe paginated movies, products, or any list API.

questions.prompt: Which cause multiple API requests during search? Select all that apply.

options.text: Fetching in onChange without debounce
Fetching when debouncedSearch changes in useEffect
Missing clearTimeout in debounce cleanup
Using controlled input with value={search}

questions.explanation: onChange fetch fires per keystroke. Missing clearTimeout lets stale timers fire. Debounced useEffect and controlled inputs are correct patterns.

questions.prompt: Cancel pending debounce timers in useEffect {{blank1}} using {{blank2}}.
questions.explanation: Every search change schedules a new timer; cleanup clears the previous one.

questions.prompt: User types 'a' then 'ab' within 400ms debounce. How many debounced values update?
questions.explanation: The first timer is cleared when search changes to 'ab'. Only one debounced update fires 400ms after the last keystroke.

questions.prompt: When debouncedSearch changes, what should happen to page state?

options.text: Keep current page — user may want page 5 of new search
Reset page to 1 — new search starts from first page
Delete page state entirely
Increment page automatically

questions.explanation: New search terms should start at page 1. Keeping page 5 of a different query returns wrong or empty results.

questions.prompt: What is the difference between debounce and throttle?
questions.sampleAnswer: Debounce waits until activity stops — ideal for search input. Throttle fires at most once per time window — ideal for scroll or resize events. Search uses debounce.
questions.explanation: Interviewers often ask this after debounce implementations.

questions.prompt: Complete the generic paginated response type.
questions.explanation: T is the type parameter representing each item in the results array.

questions.prompt: setTimeout callbacks are scheduled as which kind of task in the event loop?

options.text: Microtask — runs before next render
Macrotask — runs after current stack and microtasks
Synchronous — runs immediately
Web Worker task

questions.explanation: setTimeout is a macrotask. Promise .then handlers are microtasks. Day 11 goes deeper.
